questions.q: Find the maximum value of f(x) = x^3 - 12x^2 + 48x + 2 on the interval [0, 8].
If f(x) = 3x^2 - 2x - 5, find the equation of the tangent to the curve at the point where x = 1.
questions.answer: The equation of the tangent is y - 0 = 6(x - 1).

questions.q: If f(x) = x^2 - 4x + 1, find the critical points of f(x).
Find the local maximum and minimum of f(x) = x^3 - 6x^2 + 9x + 2.
questions.answer: The local maximum is (3 - √3, 11/2) and the local minimum is (3 + √3, 11/2).

questions.q: If f(x) = 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 1, find the point on the curve where the tangent is parallel to the x-axis.
Find the equation of the normal to the curve f(x) = 2x^2 - 3x + 1 at the point where x = 2.
questions.answer: The equation of the normal is y - 5 = -1/5(x - 2).

questions.q: If f(x) = x^2 + 2, find the x-coordinate of the point where the tangent is perpendicular to the x-axis.
Find the critical points of f(x) = x^4 - 6x^2 + 1.
If f(x) = x^3 - 6x^2 + 9x + 2, find the equation of the tangent to the curve at the point where x = 1.
questions.answer: The equation of the tangent is y - 4 = 9(x - 1).

questions.q: Find the local maximum and minimum of f(x) = x^2 - 4x + 1.
questions.answer: The local maximum is (2, -1) and the local minimum is (1, 0).
